behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a local module boundary (classifier -> API client payload), not pure single-function logic. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS/subprocess is required; a stub client that captures the outbound payload is sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: a pure unit test of prompt-builder logic could miss wiring/serialization omissions between modules. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not dependent on model output quality and no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text is about request contents ('includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels'), which can be validated before real HTTP.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with a test API client adapter that records requests; call classify(document) and assert every taxonomy label is present in the sent payload, including when taxonomy has multiple entries and edge ordering.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears at handoff/parsing boundary (API response -> classifier normalization). REAL-INFRA: no real HTTP needed; deterministic stub response with invalid label reproduces failure. MOCK-HIDE: unit-testing only the normalization helper may miss that integration path bypasses validation. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no dependence on real model quality and no chained agentic steps. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text focuses on local fallback mapping ('returns "unknown"'), which is verifiable with in-process wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Use classifier + real response-parsing path + stub API transport returning an out-of-taxonomy label; assert returned value is exactly "unknown" and raw unexpected label is never exposed.

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model behavior over repeated calls; stubs are typically deterministic and will hide nondeterminism/drift. MOCK-HIDE: mocked API would likely always return identical labels, missing true instability. REAL-INFRA: real API call is needed, but the critical failure boundary is model nondeterminism rather than transport mechanics. MULTI-STEP: this is repeated single-step calls, not a multi-agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior text explicitly requires consistency 'across repeated calls,' which is exactly what deterministic mocks cannot validate for a real LLM.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real classifications for the same fixed document and prompt settings; fail if returned labels are not all identical. Record distribution to detect intermittent label flips.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this behavior is about actual token consumption against configured budget, which is tied to real provider accounting/HTTP response usage fields and can differ from local estimates. MOCK-HIDE: mocked usage counters can always pass and miss real over-budget calls caused by provider-side tokenization or request envelope effects. LLM-DEP: not primarily about semantic quality/classification accuracy; it is usage accounting, so Agentic is not strictly required as minimum. MULTI-STEP: no chained agentic pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because behavior text targets budget compliance ('number of tokens consumed ... within budget'), not subjective/model-quality correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a real API classification for representative documents and assert reported total token usage (prompt + completion, if available) is <= configured budget; include a guard that fails on missing/invalid usage metadata.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: rate-limit handling is coupled to real HTTP/API signaling and timing behavior (e.g., 429/Retry-After/backoff wait). MOCK-MISS: simple mocked exceptions can miss failures in actual transport/status/header interpretation and sleep/retry timing. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no dependence on model output quality and no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior text requires real waiting and retry on API signal ('waits and retries'), where environment-level timing/HTTP semantics can be the real failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real HTTP test endpoint (or sandbox API) that returns rate-limit responses before success, verify classifier waits/backs off and eventually returns a label instead of surfacing immediate error; assert retry count and elapsed wait meet policy.
